text.title: Reasoning-First Architectures Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every reasoning-first-architectures spec MUST enforce.

rule.statement: Every action turn MUST be preceded by a Thought (or extended_thinking block); naked tool calls are forbidden.
rule.rationale: ReAct's mechanic; without it the agent acts on hallucinated state.

rule.statement: When task_branching == true AND solution_paths > 1, the spec MUST select Tree-of-Thought.
rule.rationale: ToT explicitly explores branches; linear ReAct cannot.

rule.statement: When error_recovery_needed == true, the spec MUST select Reflexion (self-critique + retry).
rule.rationale: Reflexion rewrites the trace summary after failure; ReAct doesn't.

rule.statement: When horizon > 20 AND branching == false, the spec MUST select Planning Loop (plan → execute → replan).
rule.rationale: Long linear horizons need replanning; ReAct wanders.

rule.statement: Every architecture MUST set max_turns and per_run_token_budget.
rule.rationale: Reasoning agents can loop indefinitely; the cap is the only honest safeguard.
